hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Digital Entrepreneurship: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 12 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #22.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Digital Entrepreneurship covers entrepreneurial skill building, idea development, product launch planning, digital marketing, and financial management for startups across 12 Sem 3-4 subjects. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at Rs 1,99,000. Best for founders, startup professionals, and intrapreneurs targeting venture-building or digital product leadership roles.

about.body:
Digital Entrepreneurship is a specialisation designed for professionals who want to build, launch, or scale digital ventures rather than climb a corporate management ladder. The Amity Online MBA in Digital Entrepreneurship covers the full startup lifecycle: entrepreneurial mindset development, opportunity identification and idea validation, digital product launch planning, and the financial management discipline that determines whether a venture survives beyond its first year.

The specialisation suits first-time founders who want to formalise their venture-building knowledge, intrapreneurs within large organisations driving new business units, and early-stage startup professionals in growth, product, or operations roles who want both the management credential and the entrepreneurial framework. It is one of the few MBA specialisations that explicitly teaches idea scouting, product launch planning, and digital customer acquisition as core subjects rather than electives.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The degree carries the Amity University name identical to the campus degree. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Developing Entrepreneurial Skills
sem3Subjects.description: Build the core competencies of entrepreneurship: opportunity mindset, risk tolerance, resource mobilisation, and founder decision-making.

sem3Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship - Basics
sem3Subjects.description: Survey the entrepreneurship ecosystem: startup types, venture lifecycle, funding stages, and the regulatory environment for new businesses in India.

sem3Subjects.name: Idea Scouting and Its Development
sem3Subjects.description: Apply systematic frameworks for identifying, evaluating, and developing business ideas from market gaps, emerging technologies, and user pain points.

sem3Subjects.name: Minor Project
sem3Subjects.description: Develop a validated business concept document: market sizing, customer interviews, competitive landscape, and initial product or service hypothesis.

sem3Subjects.name: Planning and Launching of the Product
sem3Subjects.description: Build a go-to-market plan covering product positioning, launch sequencing, channel selection, and early customer acquisition strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Professional Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Examine ethical obligations of founders: stakeholder transparency, investor relations ethics, employee treatment in resource-constrained environments, and data privacy responsibilities.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply competitive strategy frameworks to startup context: market entry, sustainable differentiation, and pivot decision criteria.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Build digital customer acquisition strategies using SEO, paid media, content marketing, and community-driven growth for digital ventures.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Aspects in Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Manage startup financial fundamentals: unit economics, burn rate, funding rounds, cap table basics, and financial projections for investor decks.

sem4Subjects.name: Major Project
sem4Subjects.description: Execute a full business plan or venture launch project: market validation, MVP definition, go-to-market strategy, financial model, and investor pitch deck.

sem4Subjects.name: Management in Action - Social Economic and Ethical Issues
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse the social impact of digital entrepreneurship: gig economy ethics, platform accountability, and responsible innovation.

sem4Subjects.name: Successful Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Design marketing systems for sustained venture growth: brand building, customer retention, and scalable distribution channel management.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core. See the main Amity MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
Digital Entrepreneurship MBA graduates from Amity take two career paths: founding their own ventures or joining early-stage startups in leadership-adjacent roles. For those launching ventures, the credential supports pitch credibility, incubator applications, and investor due diligence in India's startup ecosystem.

For those joining established organisations, the target roles include Growth Manager at Series A-B startups, Product Launch Manager at digital companies, New Ventures Manager at large corporates, and Intrapreneurship Lead at FMCG or technology companies running internal incubators. Mid-level roles include Head of New Ventures, Business Development Head at startups, and Chief Operating Officer at early-stage companies.

Key hiring contexts include Y Combinator India portfolio companies, NASSCOM startup ecosystem members, Sequoia India portfolio startups, FMCG companies with intrapreneurship programmes (Hindustan Unilever Launchpad, Tata Innoverse), and corporate venture arms of technology companies. EdTech companies, healthtech startups, and fintech platforms are also active hiring contexts for Entrepreneurship MBA graduates who understand digital product launch and growth fundamentals.

skills.body:
The Digital Entrepreneurship specialisation builds skills that founders and venture-builders apply at every stage of a startup lifecycle:

Idea Validation: structured frameworks for idea scouting, market gap analysis, and customer discovery interviews — the difference between pursuing a genuine market opportunity and building a solution without a problem. Product Launch Planning: go-to-market strategy, launch sequencing, channel prioritisation, and early customer acquisition — directly applicable to product managers and growth leads at early-stage companies. Entrepreneurial Finance: burn rate management, unit economics (CAC, LTV, payback period), funding rounds, and cap table fundamentals — the financial literacy that most founders lack and most investors test first. Digital Customer Acquisition: SEO, paid media, community marketing, and content-led growth — the practical toolkit for acquiring the first 1,000 customers on a startup budget. Strategic Positioning: competitive moat identification, market entry strategy, and pivot decision criteria — tested in investor conversations and board discussions. Marketing Systems: building repeatable marketing engines rather than one-off campaigns — applicable to founders transitioning from growth hacking to sustainable customer acquisition.

Soft skills include founder communication, investor pitch storytelling, and managing a small team through uncertainty.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Digital Entrepreneurship against peers:

Amity vs Chandigarh University Entrepreneurship MBA (/universities/chandigarh-university-online/mba): CU offers an Entrepreneurship spec at NIRF #19 and NAAC A+. CU has strong placement infrastructure but its entrepreneurship content is more generalist. Amity's explicit Idea Scouting, Planning and Launching of the Product, and Financial Aspects in Entrepreneurship subjects are more structured for early-stage venture building. For pan-India startup ecosystem credibility, Amity NIRF #22 is competitive with CU.

Amity vs LPU Entrepreneurship MBA (/universities/lovely-professional-university-online/mba): LPU has an entrepreneurship track at lower fees and NAAC A++. LPU has a strong incubation centre (LIMTEX) that active campus students access. For online LPU students, the incubation access is limited. Amity's dedicated Digital Entrepreneurship spec with digital marketing and financial aspects is more specifically oriented to digital ventures.

Amity vs Manipal Online MBA (/universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba): MAHE Online does not offer a dedicated Entrepreneurship spec. Its General Management track is broader. For candidates committed to a startup or founder career path, Amity's dedicated spec is more aligned. MAHE is stronger for corporate management roles but lacks this entrepreneurship focus.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA in Digital Entrepreneurship is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Examination fees may apply separately. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the current fee at the official Amity Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Digital Entrepreneurship) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For founders pitching to investors or applying to startup accelerators, the Amity MBA credential from a NIRF #22 university strengthens the team credibility section of a pitch deck or application.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Digital Entrepreneurship alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arjun Mehta
items.liked: Idea scouting and product launch planning modules gave structure to what I had been doing intuitively
items.disliked: Financial aspects module needed more India-specific funding landscape coverage — SEBI angel fund rules, government startup schemes
items.body: I was building my second startup while doing this MBA. The idea validation and go-to-market planning content gave me frameworks I should have had the first time around. The major project became my actual Series A pitch deck — my investors specifically asked where I learned to structure financials the way I did. The Amity brand at NIRF #22 gave the pitch credibility that a certificate course could not have provided.

items.name: Sneha Pillai
items.liked: Digital marketing module was specifically designed for startup budgets — not the large brand approach most MBAs teach
items.disliked: Cap table and equity dilution content was too brief — critical for founders but only covered at a surface level
items.body: I joined a Series A edtech startup as Growth Manager after completing this MBA. The digital customer acquisition frameworks from the digital marketing and successful marketing subjects were directly applicable to the growth role. The startup financial management content helped me understand unit economics in board meetings. The Amity degree helped me get taken seriously in a room full of IIM graduates.

items.name: Rajiv Nair
items.liked: Entrepreneurial skills module changed how I think about problems — useful even for an intrapreneur at a large company
items.disliked: Content is more suited for B2C digital startups than B2B SaaS — limited enterprise startup coverage
items.body: I lead new venture development at a large manufacturing company. The entrepreneurship and product launch content was directly applicable to how we evaluate and launch new business units. The idea scouting frameworks are now part of our internal innovation process. The Amity MBA gave my proposals more weight internally — a formal management degree from a NIRF-ranked university carries more internal credibility than a startup bootcamp certificate.

faqs.question: Is this MBA only for people starting their own business?
faqs.answer: No. Digital Entrepreneurship MBA is valuable for intrapreneurs at large organisations managing new ventures, growth and product professionals at startups, and corporate innovation managers. The venture-building skills are directly applicable in any context where you are launching something new under resource constraints, whether within an existing organisation or independently.

faqs.question: Does Amity provide incubation or funding access for MBA students?
faqs.answer: Amity has a startup incubation centre primarily supporting campus-based students. For online students, access to formal incubation programmes may be limited. Verify current online student incubation access directly with Amity Online before enrolling if incubation support is a primary goal. Supplement with government startup programmes such as Startup India, SIDBI CGTMSE, or state government incubators that do not require university affiliation.

faqs.question: What are the financial management topics covered for founders?
faqs.answer: Financial Aspects in Entrepreneurship covers startup unit economics (CAC, LTV, contribution margin), burn rate management, funding round basics (seed, Series A, B), cap table fundamentals, and financial projections for investor decks. It covers the financial discipline required at early-stage and growth-stage ventures, not corporate financial management. For deeper financial modelling, supplement with a financial modelling certification.

faqs.question: Can this MBA help with applications to startup accelerators like Y Combinator or Antler?
faqs.answer: Yes, indirectly. Accelerator applications are primarily evaluated on the strength of the idea, team execution track record, and market opportunity. The MBA provides frameworks for articulating these more clearly and a team credential that reinforces founder credibility. The Amity NIRF #22 recognition is meaningful in the Indian accelerator context. The MBA alone does not substitute for a strong idea or prior startup traction.

faqs.question: What is the salary range for Digital Entrepreneurship MBA graduates who join corporate roles?
faqs.answer: Corporate new ventures and innovation roles for MBA graduates with 2-4 years of experience typically pay Rs 8-15 lakh. Growth Manager or Product Launch Manager roles at Series A-B startups pay Rs 10-18 lakh. For founders, outcomes depend entirely on venture performance. The MBA contributes most to career earnings by improving the quality of company-building decisions rather than by qualifying for a specific salary band.

chooseIf: You are a founder, aspiring founder, or intrapreneur and want a formal management credential that explicitly covers idea validation, product launch planning, startup finance, and digital customer acquisition.
You are a startup professional in growth, product, or business development targeting a formal MBA to complement hands-on startup experience.
You want a NIRF #22, NAAC A+ credential that is explicitly oriented to venture-building rather than corporate management.
consider: You are targeting a corporate management career path rather than venture-building — General Management or a functional spec (Finance, HR, Marketing) is more relevant to corporate hiring managers.
You want deeper financial management coverage for raising institutional capital — supplement with a financial modelling or venture finance certification alongside this MBA.
You want the strongest possible MBA brand for a startup career — consider whether an IIMA PGPX or ISB YLP programme aligns with your career goals if premium brand is the primary criterion.
